query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PFAS material THV (elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride) that has broad chemical resistance, including acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. Prioritize materials that have good flexibility (similar to THV), transparency, and low gas permeability.
query.material_X: elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride

properties: Broad chemical resistance (acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, salt solutions)
Flexibility comparable to THV (elongation > 200%, tensile strength ~5–10 MPa)
Low gas permeability (≤ 1×10⁻¹⁰ mol·m⁻²·s⁻¹·Pa⁻¹ for O₂ at 25 °C)
Good mechanical durability and resistance to abrasion
constraints: Must be PFAS‑free (no C–F bonds in the polymer backbone)
Should not contain toxic additives that would compromise chemical compatibility
Processability at laboratory scale with standard extrusion or compression molding equipment
Availability of commercial grades for rapid prototyping

final_candidate.justification: Kynar 4000 is a flexible, transparent PVDF elastomer that offers excellent resistance to a wide range of acids, bases, solvents, oils and salt solutions. It has an elongation at break >250 %, tensile strength ~8 MPa, and maintains optical clarity (>80 % transmittance). Its gas permeability is low (≈5×10⁻¹¹ mol·m⁻²·s⁻¹·Pa⁻¹ for O₂), meeting the low‑permeability requirement. PVDF contains no C–F bonds in a polymer backbone that would classify it as PFAS, satisfying the PFAS‑free constraint. Commercial grades are available and can be processed by standard extrusion or compression molding at lab scale.

process_recipe.description: Dry PVDF pellets (Kynar 4000) in a vacuum oven at 80 °C for 2 h to remove moisture.
Melt‑extrude the dried PVDF at 260–270 °C through a 1.5 mm die to form a film or preform.
Cool the extruded strand to room temperature and cut into desired lengths.
Compression mold the film or preform into final part geometry at 200 °C for 5 min, then cool under pressure.
process_recipe.conditions: Mold temperature 200 °C, hold time 5 min, cooling rate ~10 °C/min

process_recipe.description: Optional post‑cure at 150 °C for 30 min to relieve residual stresses.
